title: Property
description: Real estate. Supports individual ownership, communal/family property (Nigeria), HUF coparcenary (India), government-vested land (Land Use Act), US community property, and informal/unregistered holdings.

id.description: Unique identifier for this property within the INHERIT document.

name.description: Display name for this property — a familiar label or street address. Should be recognisable to all parties involved in the estate.

propertyType.$comment: detached_house: standalone house. semi_detached_house: shares one wall with another. terraced_house: shares walls on both sides. flat: apartment within a larger building. maisonette: flat with its own entrance, typically over two floors. bungalow: single-storey dwelling. cottage: small rural dwelling. farmhouse: dwelling on agricultural land. barn_conversion: former agricultural building converted to residential. land: undeveloped plot. commercial: office, shop, warehouse, etc. mixed_use: combination of residential and commercial. houseboat: floating dwelling. mobile_home: transportable dwelling. other: any type not listed.
propertyType.description: The type of property. UK-centric terms are used as defaults — map to local equivalents as needed.

estimatedValue.description: Owner's estimate of the property's current market value, in minor currency units.

professionalValuation.description: Professional surveyor or valuer's assessment of the property value, in minor currency units.

valuationDate.description: Date the most recent valuation (estimated or professional) was performed.

valuationConfidence.$comment: estimated: owner's own estimate, no professional input. professional: assessed by a qualified surveyor or valuer. probate: valuation agreed with HMRC or equivalent tax authority for probate purposes. unknown: no information on how the value was derived.
valuationConfidence.description: Level of confidence in the stated valuation.

isPrimaryResidence.$comment: Annotation only — JSON Schema 'default' does not insert values. Consuming apps must implement defaulting.
isPrimaryResidence.description: Whether this is the testator's primary residence. Relevant for residence nil rate band (UK), homestead exemption (US), and similar tax reliefs.

ownershipType.$comment: sole: one person owns the entire property. joint_tenants: co-owners with right of survivorship — on death, the deceased's share passes automatically to the survivor(s). tenants_in_common: co-owners without survivorship — the deceased's share passes under the will or intestacy rules. trust: held by trustees for beneficiaries. tenancy_by_entirety: US spousal joint tenancy (PA, FL, MA, IL, OH, etc.) — survives creditors of the deceased spouse.
ownershipType.description: How ownership of this property is legally structured.

ownershipPercentage.$comment: Capped at 2 decimal places. For legal disputes requiring higher precision, use notes.
ownershipPercentage.description: The testator's ownership share as a percentage. For joint tenants, typically 50% for two co-owners. For tenants in common, may be any split.

ownershipModel.$comment: individual: single person owns outright. joint: two or more co-owners. communal_family: Nigerian family land or clan property — not individually owned. huf_coparcenary: Hindu Undivided Family joint property (India) — devolves by survivorship among coparceners. tribal: indigenous/tribal communal land. government_vested: Nigerian Certificate of Occupancy — land vested in the state, occupier holds a right of occupancy. trust_held: property held by trustees.
ownershipModel.description: The cultural or legal model of ownership. Goes beyond the simple legal title to capture communal and customary ownership forms.

acquisitionType.$comment: self_acquired: purchased or earned by the current owner — devolves by will or intestacy. ancestral_joint: coparcenary property that has not been partitioned — devolves by survivorship. ancestral_severed: formerly joint property that has been partitioned — devolves by will or intestacy. inherited: received through inheritance. gifted: received as a gift inter vivos. stridhan: Hindu woman's absolute property (India). communal: communal or clan property. waqf_endowed: Islamic endowment — cannot be sold or inherited.
acquisitionType.description: How the property was acquired. Determines which succession law applies in India and other jurisdictions.

tenureType.$comment: freehold: outright ownership of land and buildings in perpetuity. leasehold: right to occupy for a fixed term (common for flats in E&W). certificate_of_occupancy: Nigerian Land Use Act 1978 — all urban land vested in state governor. customary_right_of_occupancy: right to occupy under customary law. communal: communal land held by a group. government_allocated: land allocated by government but not freehold. traditional_authority_granted: land granted by a chief or traditional authority. informal_unregistered: occupation without formal title — common in developing countries.
tenureType.description: The legal basis on which the property is held. Varies dramatically by jurisdiction.

registrationStatus.$comment: formally_registered: registered with the national land registry or equivalent. informally_held: occupied but not formally registered. community_acknowledged: ownership recognised by the community but not the state. disputed: ownership is contested. undocumented: no documentation exists.
registrationStatus.description: Whether this property is formally registered with a land authority.

ownershipEvidence.$comment: title_deed: formal land registry title. certificate_of_occupancy: Nigerian Certificate of Occupancy. family_recognition: the family acknowledges ownership but no formal document exists. community_testimony: community members can attest to ownership. receipts_only: purchase receipts exist but no formal title. none: no evidence of ownership.
ownershipEvidence.description: The type of evidence available to prove ownership. In many jurisdictions, informal evidence is the only kind available.

applicableSuccessionLaw.$comment: Which succession law governs this property. Immoveables typically governed by lex rei sitae (law where the property is situated).
applicableSuccessionLaw.description: The succession law governing this specific property. For immoveable property, this is typically the law of the place where the property is situated (lex rei sitae).

jurisdiction.description: Jurisdiction whose succession law governs this property.

section.description: Specific section of the law that applies.

custodianPersonId.$comment: For communal/family property: the current custodian (not owner). On death, custodianship passes by custom, not by will.
custodianPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the current custodian of communal or family property. The custodian manages but does not own the property.

successionRule.$comment: testamentary: passes according to the will. intestacy: passes under statutory intestacy rules. customary_eldest_son: passes to the eldest son under customary law. customary_family_council: family council determines succession. customary_matrilineal: passes through the mother's line (e.g. Akan, Minangkabau). survivorship: passes automatically to surviving co-owner(s). not_individually_bequeathable: communal property that cannot be included in a will.
successionRule.description: How this property passes on the owner's death. May differ from the general estate succession rule.

governmentConsentRequired.$comment: Nigerian Land Use Act: governor's consent required for all transfers of rights. Some Indian states require similar for agricultural land.
governmentConsentRequired.description: Whether government or regulatory consent is required to transfer this property. Common in Nigeria (governor's consent) and for agricultural land in parts of India.

mobilityType.$comment: immoveable: land and buildings. moveable: personal property (rarely applies to Property entities). mixed: property with both immoveable and moveable components. Relevant for Shia inheritance (immoveable property rules differ), French succession (lex rei sitae), and halachic bekhor rules.
mobilityType.description: Whether this property is classified as immoveable, moveable, or mixed. Determines which conflict-of-laws rules apply.

mortgageOutstanding.description: Outstanding mortgage balance in minor currency units. If the property is mortgage-free, omit this field.

characterClassification.$comment: community: jointly owned marital property (50/50 in US community property states — CA, TX, AZ, WA, NV, NM, LA, ID, WI). separate: individually owned, not subject to marital property claims. quasi_community: CA-specific — property acquired in a non-community state but treated as community on divorce/death. mixed: partially community and partially separate. not_applicable: jurisdiction does not distinguish community and separate property.
characterClassification.description: Marital property character classification for US community property states. Determines whether the testator may dispose of the entire property or only their half.

homesteadStatus.$comment: Homestead exemption protects primary residence from creditors and may restrict devise. Florida: unlimited value. Texas: 200 acres rural / 10 acres urban.
homesteadStatus.description: US homestead exemption status. Protects the primary residence from creditors and may restrict the testator's ability to devise the property.

isHomestead.description: Whether this property qualifies as a homestead.

exemptionAmount.description: Dollar value of the homestead exemption, if capped.

exemptionUnlimited.$comment: Florida, Texas (rural), Iowa, Kansas, Oklahoma, South Dakota.
exemptionUnlimited.description: Whether the homestead exemption has no dollar cap. True in Florida, Texas (rural), and several other states.

deviseRestriction.$comment: Florida: cannot devise homestead away from spouse/minor children.
deviseRestriction.description: Whether this homestead is subject to restrictions on devise — e.g. Florida prohibits devising homestead away from a surviving spouse or minor children.

examples: Florida unlimited homestead — devise restricted by Art. X s.4 Florida Constitution

passesOutsideEstate.$comment: True if this property passes outside probate (JTWROS, TOD deed, trust-held). NOT distributed under the will.
passesOutsideEstate.description: Whether this property passes outside the probate estate — typically via joint tenancy with right of survivorship, a transfer-on-death deed, or a trust.

statutoryLifeEstate.$comment: Statutory life estate imposed by law (Alberta Dower Act, Florida homestead devise restriction). Not the same as a testamentary life interest.
statutoryLifeEstate.description: A life estate imposed by statute rather than by the will. The surviving spouse or other protected person has a right to occupy for life.

beneficiaryPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the life estate beneficiary.

basis.$comment: Legal basis — e.g., 'Alberta Dower Act', 'Florida Constitution Art. X s.4'
basis.description: The statutory basis for this life estate.

conditions.description: Any conditions on the life estate.
examples: Must maintain the property in habitable condition

notes.description: Free-text notes about this property. Use for unusual tenure arrangements, ongoing disputes, or planning considerations.
examples: Grade II listed building — consent required for any alterations
Boundary dispute with neighbour ongoing since 2022

images.description: Photographs and videos of this property — exterior, interior, key rooms, any damage or features relevant to valuation.
